title: Nematus spiraeae

and sometimes the **spiraea sawfly**.[[1]](#cite_note-RHS-1) It is native to central and northern Europe and was first recorded in Britain in 1924. Its larvae feed on the leaves of [goat's beard](/wiki/Aruncus_dioicus "Aruncus dioicus") (*Aruncus dioicus*).[[2]](#cite_note-Alford-2)

Alford, David V. (2008). [*Pest and Disease Management Handbook*](https://books.google.com/books?id=rxw5JvD7MN4C&pg=PA457). John Wiley & Sons. p. 457. [ISBN](/wiki/ISBN_(identifier) "ISBN (identifier)") [978-0-470-68019-3](/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-0-470-68019-3 "Special:BookSources/978-0-470-68019-3").

| Phylum: | [Arthropoda](/wiki/Arthropod "Arthropod") |

| Suborder: | [Symphyta](/wiki/Sawfly "Sawfly") |

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/5/5c/Aruncus_dioicus.jpg/250px-Aruncus_dioicus.jpg)](/wiki/File:Aruncus_dioicus.jpg)

Goat's beard (*[Aruncus dioicus](/wiki/Aruncus_dioicus "Aruncus dioicus")*)

***Nematus spiraeae*** is a species of [sawfly](/wiki/Sawfly "Sawfly") in the family [Tenthredinidae](/wiki/Tenthredinidae "Tenthredinidae"), known as the **aruncus sawfly** and sometimes the **spiraea sawfly**.[[1]](#cite_note-RHS-1) It is native to central and northern Europe and was first recorded in Britain in 1924. Its larvae feed on the leaves of [goat's beard](/wiki/Aruncus_dioicus "Aruncus dioicus") (*Aruncus dioicus*).[[2]](#cite_note-Alford-2)

## Description

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Nematus_spiraeae&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Description")]

The adult aruncus sawfly is between 5 and 6 mm (0.20 and 0.24 in) long. The antennae, head and thorax are brownish-black and the abdomen yellowish-brown, the underside being paler than the upper side. The wings are membranous with brown veins and the [tegulae](/wiki/Tegula_(insect_anatomy) "Tegula (insect anatomy)") are pale-coloured. Larvae grow to 20 mm (0.8 in) long; they are a translucent green and have short, pale hairs growing from low warts. The head is greenish-brown to brown. Eggs are capsule-shaped, white and about 1 mm (0.04 in) long.[[2]](#cite_note-Alford-2)

## Life cycle

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Nematus_spiraeae&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Life cycle")]

Aruncus sawflies are all females, breeding by [parthenogenesis](/wiki/Parthenogenesis "Parthenogenesis").[[1]](#cite_note-RHS-1) The adults emerge in late spring and [eggs](/wiki/Egg "Egg") are laid on the underside of the leaves of [goat's beard](/wiki/Aruncus_dioicus "Aruncus dioicus") (*Aruncus dioicus*) shortly afterwards. Eggs hatch a week later, releasing gregarious larvae, which consume leaf tissue between the main veins. [Metamorphosis](/wiki/Metamorphosis "Metamorphosis") occurs after four or five weeks in the soil, each pupa protected by silken [cocoons](/wiki/Pupa#Cocoon "Pupa"). Adults emerge in late July and August producing a second generation of larvae, and occasionally a third generation. Those larvae descend to the ground late in the year, overwinter as pre-pupae, and pupate in the spring.[[2]](#cite_note-Alford-2)

## Damage and control

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Nematus_spiraeae&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Damage and control")]

In heavy infestations, leaves may be skeletonized except for their main veins,[[2]](#cite_note-Alford-2) and the plants may become completely defoliated.[[3]](#cite_note-3) The larvae can be picked off by hand, or in heavier infestations, chemical pesticides can be used. Organic products such as [pyrethrum](/wiki/Pyrethrum "Pyrethrum") will control small larvae but more powerful synthetic insecticides are needed when the larvae are large.[[1]](#cite_note-RHS-1)
